concepts.name: Serving Cost Dominance Law
concepts.description: The economic principle that the ongoing operational costs of inference exceed the initial capital expenditure of training by orders of magnitude.

concepts.name: Model Sharding
concepts.description: Distributing model parameters across multiple accelerators when they exceed the memory capacity of a single device.

concepts.name: Time to First Token
concepts.description: The latency required to process an input prompt and generate the initial output token during the compute-bound prefill phase.

concepts.name: Time Per Output Token
concepts.description: The latency required to generate each subsequent token during the memory-bandwidth-bound decode phase.

concepts.name: Serving Tax
concepts.description: The cumulative latency overhead introduced by distributing inference, including network communication, serialization, and coordination.

concepts.name: Static Batching
concepts.description: Accumulating a fixed number of inference requests before dispatching them to the accelerator to maximize throughput.

concepts.name: Continuous Batching
concepts.description: Dynamically inserting new requests into a batch at each decoding iteration to prevent GPU idle time caused by variable output lengths.

concepts.name: Feature-Parallel Batching
concepts.description: Batching requests by feature type rather than whole requests to optimize sparse embedding lookups across sharded servers.

concepts.name: Pre-allocated Memory Management
concepts.description: Reserving a fixed maximum memory budget per request at admission time, often leading to significant wasted capacity.

concepts.name: Paged Memory Management
concepts.description: Allocating memory dynamically in fixed-size blocks to eliminate fragmentation and improve maximum concurrent request capacity.

concepts.name: Preemptive Scheduling
concepts.description: Pausing long-running requests and swapping their state to CPU memory to allow shorter or higher-priority requests to execute.

concepts.name: Tensor Parallelism
concepts.description: Sharding individual layer weight matrices across multiple GPUs, requiring synchronization after each layer's computation.

concepts.name: Disaggregated Serving
concepts.description: Separating the compute-bound prefill phase and memory-bound decode phase onto specialized, distinct hardware pools.

concepts.name: Stateful Serving
concepts.description: Inference systems that must persistently store and route requests to session-specific state, such as language model conversation history.

concepts.name: Batching Efficiency Curve
concepts.description: The mathematical relationship demonstrating the trade-off between batch size, latency overheads, and system throughput.

concepts.name: M/G/c/K Queue Model
concepts.description: A queuing theory model used to analyze GPU inference systems with stochastic arrival rates and general service time distributions.

concepts.name: Little's Law
concepts.description: A fundamental queuing principle relating system concurrency to the product of throughput and average latency.

concepts.name: Dynamic Batching
concepts.description: Accumulating requests up to either a maximum batch size or a maximum time window to balance throughput against strict latency targets.

concepts.name: Streaming Inference
concepts.description: Processing continuous input data incrementally as it arrives with strict real-time latency constraints instead of batching.

concepts.name: KV Cache
concepts.description: A dynamically growing memory buffer that stores previously computed attention keys and values to prevent redundant computation.

concepts.name: Internal Fragmentation
concepts.description: Memory wasted inside a pre-allocated block when a sequence generates fewer tokens than the reserved maximum capacity.

concepts.name: External Fragmentation
concepts.description: Unusable gaps of free memory created between active allocations as sequences of varying lengths complete and deallocate.

concepts.name: PagedAttention Mechanism
concepts.description: An attention implementation that stores the KV cache in non-contiguous memory pages to completely eliminate fragmentation waste.

concepts.name: Prefix Caching
concepts.description: Sharing physical memory blocks for identical initial KV cache entries across different requests, such as common system prompts.

concepts.name: KV Cache Quantization
concepts.description: Compressing the stored attention activations into lower precision formats like INT8 or INT4 to reduce memory footprint.

concepts.name: Grouped Query Attention
concepts.description: An architectural design where multiple query heads share a single key-value head, fundamentally reducing the required KV cache size.

concepts.name: Speculative Decoding
concepts.description: An algorithm where a small draft model generates candidate tokens sequentially and a large target model verifies them in parallel.

concepts.name: Test-Time Compute Scaling
concepts.description: Dynamically scaling the amount of computation spent per inference request proportional to the complexity of the task.
